toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
###############################################################################
# bulk-topic-extraction.sh
#
# Runs Sidekick topic extraction on all transcripts from ~/.claude/projects/
# and stores results in .sidekick/sessions/<session-id>/topic.json
#
# This pre-analyzes transcripts to populate intent_category and initial_goal
# metadata, making test data curation much easier.
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/bulk-topic-extraction.sh [--provider PROVIDER] [--model MODEL]
#
# Options:
#   --provider    LLM provider (default: openrouter)
#   --model       Model name (default: google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite)
#   --dry-run     Show what would be processed without running analysis
#   --force       Re-analyze even if topic.json already exists
###############################################################################

set -euo pipefail

# Parse arguments
PROVIDER="openrouter"
MODEL="google/gemini-2.5-flash-lite"
DRY_RUN=false
FORCE=false
LIMIT=0  # 0 means no limit

while [[ $# -gt 0 ]]; do
    case "$1" in
        --provider)
            PROVIDER="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        --model)
            MODEL="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        --dry-run)
            DRY_RUN=true
            shift
            ;;
        --force)
            FORCE=true
            shift
            ;;
        --limit)
            LIMIT="$2"
            shift 2
            ;;
        *)
            echo "Unknown option: $1"
            exit 1
            ;;
    esac
done

# Constants
PROJECTS_DIR="${HOME}/.claude/projects"
SIDEKICK_ROOT="src/sidekick"
SESSIONS_DIR=".sidekick/sessions"

# Counters
declare -i total_transcripts=0
declare -i processed=0
declare -i skipped=0
declare -i failed=0

###############################################################################
# Setup
###############################################################################

echo "=============================================="
echo "Bulk Topic Extraction"
echo "=============================================="
echo ""
echo "Provider: $PROVIDER"
echo "Model:    $MODEL"
echo "Mode:     $([ "$DRY_RUN" = true ] && echo "DRY RUN" || echo "LIVE")"
echo "Force:    $([ "$FORCE" = true ] && echo "YES" || echo "NO")"
echo "Limit:    $([ "$LIMIT" -gt 0 ] && echo "$LIMIT transcripts" || echo "NO LIMIT")"
echo ""

# Load .env file if it exists (for API keys)
if [ -f ".env" ]; then
    set -a  # Auto-export all variables
    source .env
    set +a
    echo "[INFO] Loaded .env file"
fi

# Verify Sidekick exists
if [ ! -d "$SIDEKICK_ROOT" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: Sidekick source not found: $SIDEKICK_ROOT"
    exit 1
fi

# Set project directory for Sidekick path resolution
export CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="$PWD"

# Load Sidekick libraries
export SIDEKICK_ROOT_OVERRIDE="$PWD/$SIDEKICK_ROOT"
source "$SIDEKICK_ROOT/lib/common.sh"

# Load topic extraction feature
if [ ! -f "$SIDEKICK_ROOT/features/topic-extraction.sh" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: topic-extraction.sh not found"
    exit 1
fi
source "$SIDEKICK_ROOT/features/topic-extraction.sh"

# Enable topic extraction feature
export FEATURE_TOPIC_EXTRACTION=true

# Override LLM configuration
export LLM_PROVIDER="$PROVIDER"
if [ "$PROVIDER" = "openrouter" ]; then
    export LLM_OPENROUTER_MODEL="$MODEL"
elif [ "$PROVIDER" = "openai-api" ]; then
    export LLM_OPENAI_MODEL="$MODEL"
elif [ "$PROVIDER" = "groq" ]; then
    export LLM_GROQ_MODEL="$MODEL"
elif [ "$PROVIDER" = "claude-cli" ]; then
    export LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL="$MODEL"
fi

log_info "Sidekick libraries loaded"
log_info "Topic extraction feature loaded"
log_info "LLM provider configured: $PROVIDER / $MODEL"

# Create sessions directory
mkdir -p "$SESSIONS_DIR"

###############################################################################
# Main Processing
###############################################################################

echo ""
echo "Scanning transcripts..."
echo ""

# Scan all projects
for project_dir in "$PROJECTS_DIR"/*; do
    [ -d "$project_dir" ] || continue

    project_name=$(basename "$project_dir")

    # Skip temporary projects
    if [[ "$project_name" == -tmp-* ]]; then
        continue
    fi

    # Scan transcripts in this project
    for transcript in "$project_dir"/*.jsonl; do
        [ -f "$transcript" ] || continue

        total_transcripts=$((total_transcripts + 1))

        # Extract session ID from filename
        session_id=$(basename "$transcript" .jsonl)
        session_dir="${SESSIONS_DIR}/${session_id}"
        topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

        # Check if already analyzed
        if [ -f "$topic_file" ] && [ "$FORCE" = false ]; then
            log_debug "Skipping $session_id (already analyzed)"
            skipped=$((skipped + 1))
            continue
        fi

        # Get transcript line count for display
        line_count=$(wc -l < "$transcript")

        echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
        echo "Session:  ${session_id:0:16}..."
        echo "Project:  ${project_name:0:40}"
        echo "Lines:    $line_count"
        echo ""

        if [ "$DRY_RUN" = true ]; then
            echo "[DRY RUN] Would analyze this transcript"
            processed=$((processed + 1))
            echo ""
            continue
        fi

        # Create session directory
        mkdir -p "$session_dir"

        # Enable debug logging for this run
        export LOG_LEVEL=DEBUG

        # Run topic extraction
        echo "Analyzing..."

        # Call the function and capture its output
        set +e  # Don't exit on error
        analysis_output=$(topic_extraction_analyze "$session_id" "$transcript" "$PWD" 2>&1)
        analysis_exit_code=$?
        set -e

        if [ $analysis_exit_code -eq 0 ]; then
            # Check if topic file was actually created
            if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
                log_info "✓ Analysis complete: $topic_file"

                # Display results
                echo ""
                echo "Results:"
                jq -r '
                    "  Intent:     " + (.intent_category // "unknown") + "\n" +
                    "  Clarity:    " + (.clarity_score | tostring) + "/10" + "\n" +
                    "  Goal:       " + ((.initial_goal // "N/A") | .[0:70]) + (if (.initial_goal | length) > 70 then "..." else "" end)
                ' "$topic_file"

                processed=$((processed + 1))
            else
                log_error "✗ Function returned success but no topic file created"
                echo "Output: $analysis_output"
                failed=$((failed + 1))
            fi
        else
            log_error "✗ Analysis failed for $session_id (exit code: $analysis_exit_code)"
            echo "Output: $analysis_output"
            failed=$((failed + 1))
        fi

        echo ""

        # Check if limit reached
        if [ "$LIMIT" -gt 0 ] && [ $((processed + failed)) -ge "$LIMIT" ]; then
            log_info "Limit reached ($LIMIT transcripts), stopping"
            break 2  # Break out of both loops
        fi

        # Add a small delay to avoid rate limiting
        sleep 0.5
    done
done

###############################################################################
# Summary
###############################################################################

echo "=============================================="
echo "Summary"
echo "=============================================="
echo "Total transcripts:    $total_transcripts"
echo "Processed:            $processed"
echo "Skipped (existing):   $skipped"
echo "Failed:               $failed"
echo ""

if [ "$DRY_RUN" = false ]; then
    echo "Topic metadata stored in: $SESSIONS_DIR/"
    echo ""
    echo "Next step:"
    echo "  ./scripts/collect-test-data.sh"
fi

_topic_extraction_write_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local llm_output="$2"

    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    log_debug "Parsing LLM output for topic extraction"

    # Extract JSON from output (handles markdown wrapping)
    local json_output
    json_output=$(llm_extract_json "$llm_output")

    if ! json_validate "$json_output"; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON from LLM output"
        return 1
    fi

    # Select appropriate snarky comment based on clarity
    local clarity
    clarity=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 5')

    local snarky_comment
    if [ "$clarity" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.high_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    else
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.low_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    fi

    # Rebuild JSON with single snarky_comment field
    json_output=$(echo "$json_output" | jq --arg snark "$snarky_comment" '. + {snarky_comment: $snark} | del(.high_clarity_snarky_comment, .low_clarity_snarky_comment)')

    # Backup existing topic.json before overwriting
    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        # Get timestamp in milliseconds (fallback to seconds if %N not supported)
        local timestamp_ms
        timestamp_ms=$(date +%s%3N 2>/dev/null || echo "$(date +%s)000")

        local backup_file="${session_dir}/${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
        mv "$topic_file" "$backup_file"
        log_debug "Backed up previous topic.json to: ${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    echo "$json_output" > "$topic_file"
    log_info "Topic file updated: $topic_file"

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PUBLIC API
# ============================================================================

# Get clarity score from topic.json
# Args: session_id
# Returns: Clarity score (1-10) or empty if not found

_topic_extraction_build_prompt() {
    local transcript_json="$1"
    local session_id="$2"

    local sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/topic-only.txt"

    if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
        log_error "Prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Read prompt template
    local prompt
    prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")

    # Load previous topic.json if exists
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
    local previous_topic=""

    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        previous_topic=$(cat "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_debug "Loaded previous topic for comparison"
    else
        log_debug "No previous topic found (first analysis)"
    fi

    # Substitute placeholders
    # Use bash parameter expansion for safer substitution
    # This handles multi-line JSON and special characters
    prompt="${prompt//\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$previous_topic}"
    prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_json}"

    echo "$prompt"
}

# Parse LLM output and write topic.json
# Args: session_id, llm_output

You are analyzing a Claude Code conversation transcript to extract the primary goal and intent.

Your task is to analyze the human/assistant conversation transcript below and provide a concise summary suitable for displaying in a statusline.

PREVIOUS TOPIC (if this session has prior analysis):
{PREVIOUS_TOPIC}

TRANSCRIPT (last few messages):
{TRANSCRIPT}

Return ONLY valid JSON matching the structure defined in topic-schema.json (no markdown, no explanation).

Field Semantics and Requirements:
- task_ids: Extract task IDs matching common patterns (T###, FEAT-###, JIRA-###, GH-###, TICKET-###, REQ-###, BUG-###, SPIKE-###, STORY-###, #123) when mentioned in active work contexts:
  * Direct imperatives: "work on X", "start X", "fix X", "implement X"
  * Current activity: "working on X", "debugging X", "stuck on X", "X is blocking"
  * Immediate planning: "next is X", "after this do X", "then tackle X"
  Exclude past-tense retrospectives ("did X", "X was done"), hypotheticals ("if X", "maybe X"), examples ("like X", "see X"), and quoted speech. Return comma-delimited list or null.
- initial_goal: Summarize the user's first stated goal. If vague/unstated early on, use snarky placeholder like "Wandering aimlessly through the codebase" or "Who knows?"
- current_objective: Summarize the most recent objective. If conversation has drifted or lost focus, use snarky observation like "Chasing squirrels" or "Lost in the weeds"
- clarity_score: 1 (vague/rambling) to 10 (crystal clear with specific requirements)
- intent_category: Pick the best fit based on user's actual goal
- high_clarity_snarky_comment: For focused conversations (clarity 7+), channel sarcastic software architect with SciFi references
- low_clarity_snarky_comment: For unclear conversations (clarity <7), offer insulting SciFi-inspired prompt to get user focused
- significant_change: If PREVIOUS_TOPIC is provided, compare task_ids, initial_goal, current_objective, intent_category, and clarity_score. Set true if any changed meaningfully (not just minor wording). Set false for first analysis or minor updates. Set true if PREVIOUS_TOPIC is empty/null.
- All fields required; task_ids and unused snarky_comment fields may be null
- Be concise but accurate

Your task is to analyze the human/assistant conversation transcript below and provide a concise summary suitable for displaying in a statusline.

PREVIOUS TOPIC (if this session has prior analysis):
{PREVIOUS_TOPIC}

TRANSCRIPT (last few messages):
{TRANSCRIPT}

Return ONLY valid JSON matching the structure defined in topic-schema.json (no markdown, no explanation).

Field Semantics and Requirements:
- task_ids: Extract task IDs matching common patterns (T###, FEAT-###, JIRA-###, GH-###, TICKET-###, REQ-###, BUG-###, SPIKE-###, STORY-###, #123) when mentioned in active work contexts:
  * Direct imperatives: "work on X", "start X", "fix X", "implement X"
  * Current activity: "working on X", "debugging X", "stuck on X", "X is blocking"
  * Immediate planning: "next is X", "after this do X", "then tackle X"
  Exclude past-tense retrospectives ("did X", "X was done"), hypotheticals ("if X", "maybe X"), examples ("like X", "see X"), and quoted speech. Return comma-delimited list or null.
- initial_goal: Summarize the user's first stated goal. If vague/unstated early on, use snarky placeholder like "Wandering aimlessly through the codebase" or "Who knows?"
- current_objective: Summarize the most recent objective. If conversation has drifted or lost focus, use snarky observation like "Chasing squirrels" or "Lost in the weeds"
- clarity_score: 1 (vague/rambling) to 10 (crystal clear with specific requirements)
- intent_category: Pick the best fit based on user's actual goal
- high_clarity_snarky_comment: For focused conversations (clarity 7+), channel sarcastic software architect with SciFi references
- low_clarity_snarky_comment: For unclear conversations (clarity <7), offer insulting SciFi-inspired prompt to get user focused
- significant_change: If PREVIOUS_TOPIC is provided, compare task_ids, initial_goal, current_objective, intent_category, and clarity_score. Set true if any changed meaningfully (not just minor wording). Set false for first analysis or minor updates. Set true if PREVIOUS_TOPIC is empty/null.
- All fields required; task_ids and unused snarky_comment fields may be null
- Be concise but accurate

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# ============================================================================
# TOPIC EXTRACTION FEATURE
# ============================================================================
# LLM-based conversation analysis for goal tracking and clarity assessment
#
# Functions:
#   topic_extraction_get_clarity()    - Get clarity score from topic.json
#   topic_extraction_analyze()        - Run LLM analysis on transcript
#   topic_extraction_check_cadence()  - Check if cadence-based analysis is due
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_start()  - Launch sleeper polling process
#   topic_extraction_sleeper_loop()   - Sleeper polling loop (background)
#
# Dependencies: lib/common.sh, prompts/*.txt
# ============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

# ============================================================================
# PRIVATE HELPERS
# ============================================================================

# Extract transcript excerpt for topic analysis
# Args: transcript_path
# Output: JSON array of preprocessed messages (configurable line count)
_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt() {
    local transcript_path="$1"

    if [ ! -f "$transcript_path" ]; then
        log_error "Transcript file not found: $transcript_path"
        echo "[]"
        return 1
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local line_count
    line_count=$(config_get "TOPIC_EXCERPT_LINES")
    line_count=${line_count:-80}

    local filter_tools
    filter_tools=$(config_get "TOPIC_FILTER_TOOL_MESSAGES")
    filter_tools=${filter_tools:-true}

    # Build jq filter for preprocessing
    local jq_filter='.message | select(. != null)'

    # Optionally filter out tool messages (tool_use and tool_result)
    if [ "$filter_tools" = "true" ]; then
        jq_filter+=' | select((.content | if type == "array" then (.[0].type != "tool_use" and .[0].type != "tool_result") else true end))'
    fi

    # Strip unnecessary attributes to reduce token usage
    jq_filter+=' | del(.model, .id, .type, .stop_reason, .stop_sequence, .usage)'

    log_debug "Extracting last $line_count lines with preprocessing (filter_tools=$filter_tools)"

    # Extract, preprocess, and wrap in array
    tail -n "$line_count" "$transcript_path" | jq -c "$jq_filter" | jq -s '.'
}

# Load prompt template and substitute transcript and previous topic
# Args: transcript_json, session_id
# Output: Prompt text with substituted transcript and previous topic
_topic_extraction_build_prompt() {
    local transcript_json="$1"
    local session_id="$2"

    local sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local prompt_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/topic-only.txt"

    if [ ! -f "$prompt_file" ]; then
        log_error "Prompt template not found: $prompt_file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Read prompt template
    local prompt
    prompt=$(cat "$prompt_file")

    # Load previous topic.json if exists
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"
    local previous_topic=""

    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        previous_topic=$(cat "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        log_debug "Loaded previous topic for comparison"
    else
        log_debug "No previous topic found (first analysis)"
    fi

    # Substitute placeholders
    # Use bash parameter expansion for safer substitution
    # This handles multi-line JSON and special characters
    prompt="${prompt//\{PREVIOUS_TOPIC\}/$previous_topic}"
    prompt="${prompt//\{TRANSCRIPT\}/$transcript_json}"

    echo "$prompt"
}

# Parse LLM output and write topic.json
# Args: session_id, llm_output
_topic_extraction_write_topic() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local llm_output="$2"

    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    log_debug "Parsing LLM output for topic extraction"

    # Extract JSON from output (handles markdown wrapping)
    local json_output
    json_output=$(llm_extract_json "$llm_output")

    if ! json_validate "$json_output"; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON from LLM output"
        return 1
    fi

    # Select appropriate snarky comment based on clarity
    local clarity
    clarity=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.clarity_score // 5')

    local snarky_comment
    if [ "$clarity" -ge 7 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.high_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    else
        snarky_comment=$(echo "$json_output" | jq -r '.low_clarity_snarky_comment // ""')
    fi

    # Rebuild JSON with single snarky_comment field
    json_output=$(echo "$json_output" | jq --arg snark "$snarky_comment" '. + {snarky_comment: $snark} | del(.high_clarity_snarky_comment, .low_clarity_snarky_comment)')

    # Backup existing topic.json before overwriting
    if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        # Get timestamp in milliseconds (fallback to seconds if %N not supported)
        local timestamp_ms
        timestamp_ms=$(date +%s%3N 2>/dev/null || echo "$(date +%s)000")

        local backup_file="${session_dir}/${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
        mv "$topic_file" "$backup_file"
        log_debug "Backed up previous topic.json to: ${timestamp_ms}-topic.json"
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    echo "$json_output" > "$topic_file"
    log_info "Topic file updated: $topic_file"

    return 0
}

# ============================================================================
# PUBLIC API
# ============================================================================

# Get clarity score from topic.json
# Args: session_id
# Returns: Clarity score (1-10) or empty if not found
topic_extraction_get_clarity() {
    local session_id="$1"

    # Get session directory (handles path resolution)
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id") || {
        log_debug "Cannot get session directory for: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    }

    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    if [ ! -f "$topic_file" ]; then
        log_debug "No topic file found for session: $session_id"
        echo ""
        return 0
    fi

    # Validate JSON first
    if ! json_validate "$(cat "$topic_file")"; then
        log_debug "Invalid JSON in topic file for session: $session_id"
        return 1
    fi

    local clarity
    clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

    echo "$clarity"
}

# Run LLM-based analysis on transcript
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success, 1 on failure
topic_extraction_analyze() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_info "Starting topic extraction analysis for session: $session_id"

    # Check if feature enabled
    if ! config_is_feature_enabled "topic_extraction"; then
        log_debug "Topic extraction feature disabled"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get model from provider-specific config
    local provider=$(config_get "LLM_PROVIDER")
    provider=${provider:-claude-cli}

    local model
    case "$provider" in
        claude-cli)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_CLAUDE_MODEL")
            model=${model:-haiku}
            ;;
        openai-api)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENAI_MODEL")
            model=${model:-gpt-4-turbo}
            ;;
        groq)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_GROQ_MODEL")
            model=${model:-openai/gpt-oss-20b}
            ;;
        openrouter)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_OPENROUTER_MODEL")
            model=${model:-google/gemma-3n-e4b-it}
            ;;
        custom)
            model=$(config_get "LLM_CUSTOM_MODEL")
            model=${model:-default}
            ;;
    esac

    log_debug "Topic analysis using model: $model"

    # Extract transcript excerpt
    local transcript_excerpt
    transcript_excerpt=$(_topic_extraction_extract_excerpt "$transcript_path")

    if [ -z "$transcript_excerpt" ] || [ "$transcript_excerpt" = "[]" ]; then
        log_warn "No transcript excerpt extracted, skipping analysis"
        return 0
    fi

    # Build prompt
    local prompt
    if ! prompt=$(_topic_extraction_build_prompt "$transcript_excerpt" "$session_id"); then
        log_error "Failed to build prompt"
        return 1
    fi

    # Load JSON schema
    local sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)
    local schema_file="${sidekick_root}/features/prompts/topic-schema.json"
    local json_schema=""

    if [ -f "$schema_file" ]; then
        json_schema=$(cat "$schema_file")
        log_debug "Loaded JSON schema from: $schema_file"
    else
        log_warn "JSON schema file not found: $schema_file (will use json_object format)"
    fi

    # Invoke LLM with schema
    log_debug "Invoking LLM for topic extraction"
    local llm_output
    if ! llm_output=$(llm_invoke "$model" "$prompt" 30 "$json_schema"); then
        log_error "LLM invocation failed"
        return 1
    fi

    # Write topic.json
    if ! _topic_extraction_write_topic "$session_id" "$llm_output"; then
        log_error "Failed to write topic file"
        return 1
    fi

    # Check if resume generation is needed
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local topic_file="${session_dir}/topic.json"

    local significant_change=$(jq -r '.significant_change // false' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "false")
    local clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")

    log_debug "significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity"

    if [ "$significant_change" = "true" ] && [ "$clarity" -ge 5 ] 2>/dev/null; then
        log_info "Triggering resume generation (significant change detected, clarity=$clarity)"
        resume_generate_async "$session_id" "$transcript_path" || {
            log_warn "Failed to launch resume generation (non-fatal)"
        }
    else
        log_debug "Resume generation not needed (significant_change=$significant_change, clarity=$clarity)"
    fi

    log_info "Topic extraction analysis complete"
    return 0
}

# Check if cadence-based analysis is due
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir, response_count
# Returns: 0 if analysis should run, 1 if not
# Side effect: Launches background analysis if due
topic_extraction_check_cadence() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"
    local response_count="$4"

    log_debug "Checking cadence for response count: $response_count"

    # Get current clarity score
    local clarity
    clarity=$(topic_extraction_get_clarity "$session_id")

    # Default to low clarity if no topic file
    if [ -z "$clarity" ]; then
        clarity=5
    fi

    # Get cadence thresholds
    local clarity_threshold=$(config_get "TOPIC_CLARITY_THRESHOLD")
    clarity_threshold=${clarity_threshold:-7}

    local high_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_HIGH")
    high_cadence=${high_cadence:-10}

    local low_cadence=$(config_get "TOPIC_CADENCE_LOW")
    low_cadence=${low_cadence:-1}

    # Determine which cadence to use
    local cadence=$low_cadence
    if [ "$clarity" -ge "$clarity_threshold" ] 2>/dev/null; then
        cadence=$high_cadence
    fi

    log_debug "Clarity: $clarity, threshold: $clarity_threshold, cadence: $cadence"

    # Check if analysis is due
    if [ $((response_count % cadence)) -eq 0 ]; then
        log_info "Cadence-based analysis due at count $response_count (cadence: $cadence)"

        # Launch analysis in background directly
        local session_dir
        session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
        local pid_file="${session_dir}/analysis.pid"
        local log_file="${session_dir}/analysis.log"

        # Check if already running
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Analysis already running for session ${session_id}"
            return 0
        fi

        log_debug "Launching analysis process for session ${session_id}"

        local sidekick_root
        sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

        nohup bash -c "
            # Source common and feature libraries
            source '$sidekick_root/lib/common.sh'
            source '$sidekick_root/features/topic-extraction.sh'

            # Re-load config
            config_load

            # Re-initialize logging
            log_init '$session_id'

            # Run analysis
            topic_extraction_analyze '$session_id' '$transcript_path' '$project_dir'
        " > /dev/null 2>&1 &

        local pid=$!
        echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
        log_debug "Launched analysis with PID ${pid}"

        return 0
    fi

    log_debug "Analysis not due (count: $response_count, cadence: $cadence)"
    return 1
}

# Launch sleeper polling process in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path, project_dir
# Returns: 0 on success
topic_extraction_sleeper_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    # Check if sleeper enabled
    local sleeper_enabled=$(config_get "SLEEPER_ENABLED")
    sleeper_enabled=${sleeper_enabled:-true}

    if [ "$sleeper_enabled" != "true" ]; then
        log_debug "Sleeper disabled, skipping"
        return 0
    fi

    # Check if sleeper already running
    local session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")
    local pid_file="${session_dir}/sleeper.pid"

    if [ -f "$pid_file" ]; then
        if process_is_running "$pid_file"; then
            log_debug "Sleeper already running for session: $session_id"
            return 0
        else
            # Stale PID file
            log_debug "Removing stale sleeper PID file"
            rm -f "$pid_file"
        fi
    fi

    log_info "Launching sleeper process for session: $session_id"

    # Get paths
    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    local script_path="${sidekick_root}/features/scripts/sleeper-loop.sh"

    # Verify script exists
    if [ ! -f "$script_path" ]; then
        log_error "Sleeper loop script not found: $script_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Launch script in background with environment variables
    nohup env \
        _SIDEKICK_ROOT="$sidekick_root" \
        CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" \
        bash "$script_path" "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir" "$sidekick_root" \
        > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    echo "$pid" > "$pid_file"
    log_debug "Launched sleeper with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

# Launch resume generation in background
# Args: session_id, transcript_path
# Returns: 0 on success
resume_generate_async() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"

    log_info "Launching resume generation for session: $session_id"

    # Get paths
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local sidekick_root
    sidekick_root=$(path_get_sidekick_root)

    local script_path="${sidekick_root}/features/scripts/generate-resume.sh"

    # Verify script exists
    if [ ! -f "$script_path" ]; then
        log_error "Resume generation script not found: $script_path"
        return 1
    fi

    # Launch script in background with environment variables
    nohup env \
        _SIDEKICK_ROOT="$sidekick_root" \
        CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" \
        bash "$script_path" "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$session_dir" "$sidekick_root" \
        > /dev/null 2>&1 &

    local pid=$!
    log_debug "Launched resume generation with PID ${pid}"

    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLUGIN HOOKS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit - UserPromptSubmit hook for topic extraction
#
# Ensures the sleeper process is running (watchdog pattern)
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - transcript_path
#   $3 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
topic_extraction_on_user_prompt_submit() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local transcript_path="$2"
    local project_dir="$3"

    log_debug "Topic Extraction: UserPromptSubmit hook"

    # Ensure sleeper is running (watchdog pattern)
    # sleeper_start checks if already running and returns early if so
    if ! topic_extraction_sleeper_start "$session_id" "$transcript_path" "$project_dir"; then
        log_warn "Failed to ensure sleeper is running (non-fatal)"
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Topic extraction feature loaded"

**Perfect!** Fixed and verified. Here's what I changed:

## Changes Made:

### 1. **topic-schema.json** - Removed unnecessary fields
- ❌ Removed `session_id` from properties and required array
- ❌ Removed `timestamp` from properties and required array
- These are now injected by the code instead of forcing the LLM to make them up

### 2. **topic-only.txt** - Added missing field documentation
- ✅ Added explicit `confidence` field description (0.0-1.0)

### 3. **topic-extraction.sh** - Inject real values
- ✅ Updated `_topic_extraction_write_topic()` to inject actual `session_id` and current `timestamp` using `jq`

### 4. **resume-schema.json & generate-resume.txt**
- ✅ Already perfectly aligned - no changes needed

## Verification:
- **Directory**: `7fb61bfe-c741-4649-829a-1585d63da38c`
- **session_id in JSON**: `"7fb61bfe-c741-4649-829a-1585d63da38c"` ✅ **Matches!**
- **timestamp**: `"2025-10-27T22:25:06Z"` ✅ **Accurate!**

        .message |
        select(. != null) |
        select(
            (.content |
                if type == "array" then
                    (.[0].type != "tool_use" and .[0].type != "tool_result")
                else
                    true
                end
            )
        ) |
        del(.model, .id, .type, .stop_reason, .stop_sequence, .usage)
    ' "$transcript" 2>/dev/null
}

transcript="$1"
echo "=== FIRST 5 MESSAGES ==="
count=0
while IFS= read -r message_json; do
    [[ -z "$message_json" ]] && continue
    
    role=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.role // "unknown"')
    content_type=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.content | type')
    
    if [[ "$content_type" == "array" ]]; then
        content=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.content[] | select(.type == "text") | .text' | head -1)

        content=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.content')

    [[ -z "$content" || "$content" == "null" ]] && continue
    [[ "$content" =~ ^\<(command|local-command|tool) ]] && continue
    [[ "$content" =~ ^Caveat:.*running\ local\ commands ]] && continue
    [[ ${#content} -lt 10 ]] && continue
    
    if [[ ${#content} -gt 150 ]]; then
        content="${content:0:147}..."
    fi
    
    echo "  ${role^}: ${content}"
    count=$((count + 1))
    [[ $count -ge 5 ]] && break
done < <(preprocess_transcript "$transcript")

echo ""

**My Assessment:**

The topic extraction is **WRONG**. Here's why:

1. **initial_goal is misleading**: The user didn't just want to "search web for docs" - they wanted to "work on the status line". The doc search was just the *first step*, not the goal.

2. **current_objective is also wrong**: "Obtain documentation" is an intermediate step. The real objective is to understand/improve/work on the status line configuration.

The clarity score reveals a fundamental misunderstanding. The conversation demonstrates significant confusion and multiple failed documentation retrieval attempts, which contradicts a high clarity rating. The extraction missed the nuanced development context, instead fixating on surface-level search actions.

The interaction exposes a critical gap between mechanical task interpretation and genuine user intent - highlighting how narrow, action-focused parsing can miss the broader collaborative development goal.

content.text:
**My Assessment: The topic extraction is incorrect.**

## What the LLM extracted:
- **initial_goal**: "Search web for Claude Code status line configuration docs."
- **current_objective**: "Obtain Claude Code status line configuration documentation."
- **intent_category**: "research"
- **clarity_score**: 7

## What the transcript actually shows:

**User's opening**: "We're going to **work on the status line** we've got configured here. **First**, search the web for the latest complete documentation..."

The LLM got tunnel vision on the word "First" and treated the documentation search as the primary goal, when it was clearly just **step 1** of a larger development task.

## Problems:

1. **initial_goal is wrong**: Should be something like "Work on/improve the status line configuration" - the doc search was just prep work

2. **current_objective is wrong**: "Obtain documentation" is a means to an end, not the objective. The real objective is working on the status line itself.

3. **intent_category is wrong**: Should be "development" not "research" - the user explicitly said "we're going to **work on**" not "tell me about"

4. **clarity_score might be generous**: A score of 7 suggests good clarity, but the conversation shows multiple failed attempts to access docs, which suggests the path forward wasn't crystal clear

## The Real Goal:
User wanted to work on/improve/understand their existing status line configuration, with fetching documentation as an initial research step. This is **development with research prep**, not pure research.

This is a good test case showing how the LLM can miss the forest for the trees when users provide step-by-step instructions.

  * Immediate planning: "next is X", "after this do X", "then tackle X"

- current_objective: Summarize the most recent objective. If conversation has drifted or lost focus, use snarky observation like "Chasing squirrels" or "Lost in the weeds"

content.thinking:
Looking at the prompt, I can see several issues that likely led to the misclassification:

1. **Line 19**: `initial_goal: Summarize the user's first stated goal` - This is too literal. It doesn't guide the LLM to distinguish between preparatory steps and the actual goal. In the statusline case, the "first stated" thing was literally "search the web", but that wasn't the goal - it was just step 1.

2. **Line 22**: `intent_category: Pick the best fit based on user's actual goal` - Very vague. Doesn't help the LLM understand what "actual goal" means when there are multiple actions mentioned.

3. **No guidance on distinguishing**:
   - Primary goals vs. preparatory steps
   - End objectives vs. means to achieve them
   - "Let's work on X by doing Y" vs "Let's do Y"

4. **No examples** showing how to handle common patterns like:
   - "First, do X... [then work on the real thing]"
   - "Before we Y, let's X"
   - "We're going to work on Z. First, research..."

The prompt needs to explicitly tell the LLM to:
1. Look for verbs like "work on", "improve", "build", "fix" as indicators of the real goal
2. Treat "first", "before", "prep", "research" as indicators of preliminary steps
3. When user says "we're going to work on X. First, do Y", X is the goal, not Y
4. Ask "what is the user ultimately trying to accomplish?" not "what did the user mention first?"

Let me suggest improvements.

content.text:
Yes, absolutely. The prompt has critical gaps that led to the misclassification. Here are the main issues:

## Problems I See:

### 1. **Line 19 - Too Literal**
```
initial_goal: Summarize the user's first stated goal.
```
This encourages literal reading. In the statusline case, the "first stated" action was "search the web", but that wasn't the **goal** - it was a preparatory step.

### 2. **No Guidance on Means vs. Ends**
The prompt doesn't help distinguish:
- **Primary objective** ("work on the status line") 
- vs. **Preparatory steps** ("First, search docs")
- vs. **Means to an end** ("fetch documentation" to enable the real work)

### 3. **Missing Pattern Recognition**
Common phrases that signal the real goal vs prep work:
- "We're going to **work on** X. First, do Y" → X is the goal
- "Let's **improve/fix/build** X by researching Y" → X is the goal
- "Before we Z, let's Y" → Z is probably the goal

### 4. **intent_category Guidance Too Vague**
```
intent_category: Pick the best fit based on user's actual goal
```
Doesn't help LLM understand "actual" when there are multiple actions. Should guide: look for verbs like "build", "work on", "improve", "debug" vs "research", "learn about", "understand".

## Suggested Improvements:

- current_objective: Summarize the most recent objective. If conversation has drifted or lost focus, use snarky observation like "Chasing squirrels" or "Lost in the weeds"
- clarity_score: 1 (vague/rambling) to 10 (crystal clear with specific requirements)
- intent_category: Pick the best fit based on user's actual goal

If user says "We're going to work on X. First, do Y", X is the goal, not Y. Distinguish preparatory actions ("research", "fetch docs", "understand") from end objectives. If truly vague/unstated, use snarky placeholder like "Wandering aimlessly through the codebase" or "Who knows?"
- current_objective: Identify what the user is trying to accomplish RIGHT NOW in the most recent messages. This should reflect the current work, which may be a preparatory step toward the initial_goal. If conversation has drifted or lost focus, use snarky observation like "Chasing squirrels" or "Lost in the weeds"
- clarity_score: 1 (vague/rambling) to 10 (crystal clear with specific requirements)
- intent_category: Classify based on the PRIMARY goal (initial_goal), not intermediate steps. "development" = building/modifying/fixing code. "research" = ONLY if the user wants to understand/learn something with no coding goal. "debugging" = actively troubleshooting failures. If user says "research X to help with Y development", it's "development"

#!/bin/bash

if [ -f "$topic_file" ]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Results:"
    jq '{ initial_goal, current_objective, intent_category, clarity_score, confidence }' "$topic_file"
fi
EOF

- intent_category: "research" ❌ **Still wrong**

You are analyzing a Claude Code conversation transcript to extract the primary goal and intent.

Your task is to analyze the human/assistant conversation transcript below and provide a concise summary suitable for displaying in a statusline.

PREVIOUS TOPIC (if this session has prior analysis):
{PREVIOUS_TOPIC}

TRANSCRIPT (last few messages):
{TRANSCRIPT}

Return ONLY valid JSON matching the structure defined in topic-schema.json (no markdown, no explanation).

Field Semantics and Requirements:
- task_ids: Extract task IDs matching common patterns (T###, FEAT-###, JIRA-###, GH-###, TICKET-###, REQ-###, BUG-###, SPIKE-###, STORY-###, #123) when mentioned in active work contexts:
  * Direct imperatives: "work on X", "start X", "fix X", "implement X"
  * Current activity: "working on X", "debugging X", "stuck on X", "X is blocking"
  * Immediate planning: "next is X", "after this do X", "then tackle X"
  Exclude past-tense retrospectives ("did X", "X was done"), hypotheticals ("if X", "maybe X"), examples ("like X", "see X"), and quoted speech. Return comma-delimited list or null.
- initial_goal: Identify the user's PRIMARY goal - what they ultimately want to accomplish, not preliminary steps. Look for verbs like "work on", "build", "improve", "fix", "implement", "refactor". If user says "We're going to work on X. First, do Y", X is the goal, not Y. Distinguish preparatory actions ("research", "fetch docs", "understand") from end objectives. If truly vague/unstated, use snarky placeholder like "Wandering aimlessly through the codebase" or "Who knows?"
- current_objective: Identify what the user is trying to accomplish RIGHT NOW in the most recent messages. This should reflect the current work, which may be a preparatory step toward the initial_goal. If conversation has drifted or lost focus, use snarky observation like "Chasing squirrels" or "Lost in the weeds"
- clarity_score: 1 (vague/rambling) to 10 (crystal clear with specific requirements)
- intent_category: Classify based on the PRIMARY goal (initial_goal), not intermediate steps. "development" = building/modifying/fixing code. "research" = ONLY if the user wants to understand/learn something with no coding goal. "debugging" = actively troubleshooting failures. If user says "research X to help with Y development", it's "development"
- confidence: 0.0 to 1.0 indicating confidence in your analysis (consider transcript length, clarity, and context completeness)
- high_clarity_snarky_comment: For focused conversations (clarity 7+), channel sarcastic software architect with SciFi references
- low_clarity_snarky_comment: For unclear conversations (clarity <7), offer insulting SciFi-inspired prompt to get user focused
- significant_change: If PREVIOUS_TOPIC is provided, compare task_ids, initial_goal, current_objective, intent_category, and clarity_score. Set true if any changed meaningfully (not just minor wording). Set false for first analysis or minor updates. Set true if PREVIOUS_TOPIC is empty/null.
- All fields required; task_ids and unused snarky_comment fields may be null
- Be concise but accurate

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
# Sidekick Feature: Resume
# Session continuity using resume.json from previous sessions

# Prevent double-sourcing
[[ -n "${_SIDEKICK_FEATURE_RESUME_LOADED:-}" ]] && return 0
readonly _SIDEKICK_FEATURE_RESUME_LOADED=1

# This file should be sourced, not executed
if [ "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}" -ef "$0" ]; then
    echo "ERROR: This file should be sourced, not executed" >&2
    exit 1
fi

# Requires lib/common.sh to be loaded
if [[ -z "${_SIDEKICK_COMMON_LOADED:-}" ]]; then
    echo "ERROR: lib/common.sh must be loaded before resume.sh" >&2
    exit 1
fi

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# resume_snarkify - Initialize current session topic from previous resume.json
#
# Finds the most recent previous session with resume.json (clarity >= threshold)
# and uses it to create the initial topic.json for the current session.
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id (current session)
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success or skip, 1 on failure
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
resume_snarkify() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    if [ -z "$session_id" ] || [ -z "$project_dir" ]; then
        log_error "resume_snarkify: session_id and project_dir are required"
        return 1
    fi

    log_info "Starting resume initialization for session: $session_id"

    # Get session directory
    local session_dir
    session_dir=$(path_get_session_dir "$session_id")

    local current_topic="$session_dir/topic.json"

    # Skip if current topic already exists
    if [ -f "$current_topic" ]; then
        log_debug "Current session topic already exists, skipping resume"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get sessions directory (requires CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR)
    if [ -z "${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR:-}" ]; then
        log_debug "CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR not set, cannot resume"
        return 0
    fi

    local sessions_dir="${CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR}/.sidekick/sessions"

    log_debug "Searching for previous resume.json in: $sessions_dir"

    # Ensure sessions directory exists
    if [ ! -d "$sessions_dir" ]; then
        log_debug "No sessions directory found, cannot resume"
        return 0
    fi

    # Get configuration
    local min_clarity
    min_clarity=$(config_get "RESUME_MIN_CLARITY")
    [ -z "$min_clarity" ] && min_clarity=5

    log_debug "Resume config: min_clarity=$min_clarity"

    # Find most recent resume.json from OTHER sessions with clarity >= threshold
    # Search all session directories except current one
    local last_resume
    last_resume=$(find "$sessions_dir" -mindepth 1 -maxdepth 1 -type d 2>/dev/null | \
        grep -v "/${session_id}$" | \
        while read -r dir; do
            local resume_file="$dir/resume.json"
            local topic_file="$dir/topic.json"

            # Must have both resume.json and topic.json
            [ -f "$resume_file" ] || continue
            [ -f "$topic_file" ] || continue

            echo "$topic_file|$resume_file"
        done | \
        while read -r pair; do
            local topic_file="${pair%%|*}"
            local resume_file="${pair##*|}"

            local clarity timestamp
            clarity=$(jq -r '.clarity_score // 0' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            timestamp=$(jq -r '.timestamp // ""' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")

            # Use file modification time if timestamp is missing
            if [ -z "$timestamp" ]; then
                timestamp=$(stat -c %Y "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || stat -f %m "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
            fi

            if [ "$clarity" -ge "$min_clarity" ] && [ -n "$timestamp" ]; then
                echo "${timestamp}|${resume_file}"
            fi
        done | sort -r | head -1 | cut -d'|' -f2)

    if [ -z "$last_resume" ] || [ ! -f "$last_resume" ]; then
        log_debug "No previous session found with resume.json (clarity >= $min_clarity)"
        return 0
    fi

    log_info "Found previous resume: $last_resume"

    # Read resume.json
    local resume_content
    if ! resume_content=$(cat "$last_resume" 2>/dev/null); then
        log_error "Failed to read resume file: $last_resume"
        return 1
    fi

    # Validate JSON
    if ! echo "$resume_content" | jq empty 2>/dev/null; then
        log_error "Invalid JSON in resume file: $last_resume"
        return 1
    fi

    # Extract fields from resume.json (using new field names)
    local last_task_id resume_last_goal_message last_objective_in_progress snarky_comment
    last_task_id=$(echo "$resume_content" | jq -r '.last_task_id // null')
    resume_last_goal_message=$(echo "$resume_content" | jq -r '.resume_last_goal_message // ""')
    last_objective_in_progress=$(echo "$resume_content" | jq -r '.last_objective_in_progress // ""')
    snarky_comment=$(echo "$resume_content" | jq -r '.snarky_comment // ""')

    # Validate required fields
    if [ -z "$resume_last_goal_message" ] || [ -z "$last_objective_in_progress" ]; then
        log_warn "Missing required fields in resume.json, skipping resume"
        return 0
    fi

    # Map to topic.json schema (convert single task_id to task_ids field)
    local task_ids_json
    if [ "$last_task_id" = "null" ] || [ -z "$last_task_id" ]; then
        task_ids_json="null"
    else
        # Wrap single task ID in string format for task_ids field
        task_ids_json="\"$last_task_id\""
    fi

    # Create initial topic.json from resume (mapping resume fields to topic schema)
    cat > "$current_topic" <<EOF
{
  "session_id": "$session_id",
  "timestamp": "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")",
  "task_ids": $task_ids_json,
  "initial_goal": "$resume_last_goal_message",
  "current_objective": "$last_objective_in_progress",
  "intent_category": "development",
  "clarity_score": 6,
  "confidence": 0.75,
  "snarky_comment": "$snarky_comment",
  "resume_from_session": true
}
EOF

    log_info "Created resume topic file: $current_topic"
    return 0
}

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# PLUGIN HOOKS
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------

#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
# resume_on_session_start - SessionStart hook for resume feature
#
# Initializes the current session topic from previous session's resume.json
#
# Arguments:
#   $1 - session_id
#   $2 - project_dir
#
# Returns:
#   0 on success (non-fatal errors logged)
#------------------------------------------------------------------------------
resume_on_session_start() {
    local session_id="$1"
    local project_dir="$2"

    log_debug "Resume: SessionStart hook"

    if ! resume_snarkify "$session_id" "$project_dir"; then
        log_warn "Failed to generate resume topic (non-fatal)"
    fi

    return 0
}

log_debug "Resume feature loaded"

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/bin/bash
###############################################################################
# collect-test-data.sh
#
# Interactive script to collect diverse transcript samples for LLM benchmarking.
# Scans ~/.claude/projects/, displays stats, and prompts user to include/skip
# each transcript. Generates metadata.json with test case classifications.
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/collect-test-data.sh [--reset]
#
# Options:
#   --reset  Clear existing test data and start fresh
###############################################################################

set -euo pipefail

# Directories
PROJECTS_DIR="${HOME}/.claude/projects"
SIDEKICK_SESSIONS_DIR=".sidekick/sessions"
OUTPUT_DIR="test-data/transcripts"
METADATA_FILE="${OUTPUT_DIR}/metadata.json"

# Counters for distribution tracking
declare -i count_short=0
declare -i count_medium=0
declare -i count_long=0
declare -i count_total=0

# Array to store metadata entries
declare -a metadata_entries=()

###############################################################################
# Helper Functions
###############################################################################

# Print colored output
print_header() {
    echo ""
    echo "=============================================="
    echo "$1"
    echo "=============================================="
}

print_info() {
    echo "[INFO] $1"
}

print_stat() {
    printf "  %-20s %s\n" "$1:" "$2"
}

print_success() {
    echo "[✓] $1"
}

print_warning() {
    echo "[!] $1"
}

# Classify transcript by length
classify_length() {
    local lines=$1
    if (( lines < 50 )); then
        echo "short"
    elif (( lines <= 150 )); then
        echo "medium"
    else
        echo "long"
    fi
}

# Extract intent category from sidekick topic.json if available
extract_intent() {
    local session_id=$1
    local topic_file="${SIDEKICK_SESSIONS_DIR}/${session_id}/topic.json"

    if [[ -f "$topic_file" ]]; then
        # Try to extract intent_category field
        local intent=$(jq -r '.intent_category // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        if [[ -n "$intent" && "$intent" != "null" ]]; then
            echo "$intent"
            return
        fi
    fi
    echo "unknown"
}

# Extract initial goal from sidekick topic.json if available
extract_goal() {
    local session_id=$1
    local topic_file="${SIDEKICK_SESSIONS_DIR}/${session_id}/topic.json"

    if [[ -f "$topic_file" ]]; then
        local goal=$(jq -r '.initial_goal // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        if [[ -n "$goal" && "$goal" != "null" ]]; then
            echo "$goal"
            return
        fi
    fi
    echo ""
}

# Extract project name from encoded directory name
extract_project_name() {
    local dir_name=$1
    # Remove leading dash and convert remaining dashes to slashes for readability
    # E.g., "-home-scott-projects-claude-config" -> "home/scott/projects/claude-config"
    echo "${dir_name#-}" | sed 's/-/\//g'
}

# Preprocess transcript using same logic as topic-extraction.sh
# Filters tool messages and strips unnecessary attributes
preprocess_transcript() {
    local transcript=$1

    # Replicate topic-extraction.sh preprocessing:
    # 1. Extract .message field (ignore wrapper metadata)
    # 2. Filter out tool_use and tool_result content
    # 3. Strip model, id, type, stop_reason, stop_sequence, usage attributes
    jq -c '
        .message |
        select(. != null) |
        select(
            (.content |
                if type == "array" then
                    (.[0].type != "tool_use" and .[0].type != "tool_result")
                else
                    true
                end
            )
        ) |
        del(.model, .id, .type, .stop_reason, .stop_sequence, .usage)
    ' "$transcript" 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract conversation preview from preprocessed transcript
# Shows first 10 lines of cleansed messages
extract_conversation_preview() {
    local transcript=$1
    local preview=""
    local msg_count=0
    local max_messages=10

    # Preprocess and extract text
    while IFS= read -r message_json; do
        [[ -z "$message_json" ]] && continue

        # Extract role and content from preprocessed message
        local role=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.role // "unknown"')
        local content=""

        # Handle both string and array content
        local content_type=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.content | type')
        if [[ "$content_type" == "array" ]]; then
            # Extract text from array content (filter text type only)
            content=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.content[] | select(.type == "text") | .text' | head -1)
        else
            # String content
            content=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.content')
        fi

        [[ -z "$content" || "$content" == "null" ]] && continue

        # Skip meta/command messages by content pattern
        [[ "$content" =~ ^\<(command|local-command|tool) ]] && continue
        [[ "$content" =~ ^Caveat:.*running\ local\ commands ]] && continue
        [[ ${#content} -lt 10 ]] && continue  # Skip very short messages

        # Capitalize role
        local display_role="${role^}"

        # Truncate long lines
        if [[ ${#content} -gt 90 ]]; then
            content="${content:0:87}..."
        fi

        preview+="      ${display_role}: ${content}"$'\n'
        msg_count=$((msg_count + 1))

        [[ $msg_count -ge $max_messages ]] && break
    done < <(preprocess_transcript "$transcript")

    echo "$preview"
}

# Generate a unique ID for the test case
generate_test_id() {
    local length_category=$1
    local counter=$2
    printf "%s-%03d" "$length_category" "$counter"
}

# Add a transcript to the collection
add_transcript() {
    local session_id=$1
    local session_dir=$2
    local transcript_file=$3
    local line_count=$4
    local length_category=$5
    local intent_category=$6
    local description=$7

    # Generate unique test ID
    local test_id
    case "$length_category" in
        short) test_id=$(generate_test_id "short" $((++count_short))) ;;
        medium) test_id=$(generate_test_id "medium" $((++count_medium))) ;;
        long) test_id=$(generate_test_id "long" $((++count_long))) ;;
    esac

    # Copy transcript to output directory
    local dest_file="${test_id}.jsonl"
    cp "$transcript_file" "${OUTPUT_DIR}/${dest_file}"

    # Create metadata entry
    local timestamp=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    local entry=$(cat <<JSON
    {
      "id": "${test_id}",
      "file": "${dest_file}",
      "source_session": "${session_id}",
      "length_category": "${length_category}",
      "line_count": ${line_count},
      "intent_category": "${intent_category}",
      "description": "${description}",
      "collected_at": "${timestamp}"
    }
JSON
    )

    metadata_entries+=("$entry")
    count_total=$((count_total + 1))

    print_success "Added as ${test_id} (${length_category}, ${intent_category})"
}

# Generate final metadata.json
write_metadata() {
    print_info "Generating metadata.json..."

    # Join entries with commas
    local entries_json=""
    for i in "${!metadata_entries[@]}"; do
        if [[ $i -gt 0 ]]; then
            entries_json+=","
        fi
        entries_json+="${metadata_entries[$i]}"
    done

    # Write complete JSON
    cat > "$METADATA_FILE" <<JSON
{
  "dataset_version": "1.0",
  "generated_at": "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")",
  "test_count": ${count_total},
  "distribution": {
    "short": ${count_short},
    "medium": ${count_medium},
    "long": ${count_long}
  },
  "transcripts": [
${entries_json}
  ]
}
JSON

    print_success "Metadata written to ${METADATA_FILE}"
}

# Display collection summary
show_summary() {
    print_header "Collection Summary"
    print_stat "Total transcripts" "$count_total"
    print_stat "Short (<50 lines)" "$count_short ($(( count_short * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))%)"
    print_stat "Medium (50-150)" "$count_medium ($(( count_medium * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))%)"
    print_stat "Long (>150 lines)" "$count_long ($(( count_long * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))%)"
    echo ""

    # Check for balanced distribution
    local target_low=25
    local target_high=40
    local pct_short=$(( count_short * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))
    local pct_medium=$(( count_medium * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))
    local pct_long=$(( count_long * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))

    if (( pct_short < target_low || pct_short > target_high )); then
        print_warning "Short transcripts: ${pct_short}% (target: 30-35%)"
    fi
    if (( pct_medium < target_low || pct_medium > target_high )); then
        print_warning "Medium transcripts: ${pct_medium}% (target: 30-40%)"
    fi
    if (( pct_long < target_low || pct_long > target_high )); then
        print_warning "Long transcripts: ${pct_long}% (target: 30-35%)"
    fi

    if (( count_total >= 20 && count_total <= 30 )); then
        print_success "Good sample size (20-30 recommended)"
    elif (( count_total < 20 )); then
        print_warning "Consider collecting more samples (20-30 recommended)"
    else
        print_info "Large sample set (${count_total} transcripts)"
    fi
}

###############################################################################
# Main Script
###############################################################################

main() {
    print_header "LLM Benchmark Test Data Collection"

    # Check for --reset flag
    if [[ "${1:-}" == "--reset" ]]; then
        print_warning "Resetting test data directory..."
        rm -f "${OUTPUT_DIR}"/*.jsonl "$METADATA_FILE"
        print_success "Existing test data cleared"
    fi

    # Verify projects directory exists
    if [[ ! -d "$PROJECTS_DIR" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: Projects directory not found: $PROJECTS_DIR"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Verify output directory exists
    mkdir -p "$OUTPUT_DIR"

    # Check for existing metadata
    if [[ -f "$METADATA_FILE" ]]; then
        print_warning "Existing metadata.json found. Use --reset to start fresh."
        read -p "Continue and append? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
        echo
        if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
            echo "Aborted."
            exit 0
        fi
    fi

    print_info "Scanning projects directory: $PROJECTS_DIR"
    echo ""

    # Scan projects
    local session_count=0
    local processed=0

    for project_dir in "$PROJECTS_DIR"/*; do
        [[ -d "$project_dir" ]] || continue

        local project_name=$(basename "$project_dir")

        # Skip temporary Claude CLI projects
        if [[ "$project_name" == -tmp-* ]]; then
            continue
        fi

        local readable_project=$(extract_project_name "$project_name")

        # Scan transcripts in this project
        for transcript in "$project_dir"/*.jsonl; do
            [[ -f "$transcript" ]] || continue

            session_count=$((session_count + 1))

            # Extract session ID from filename (e.g., "51e06a7f-160e-4991-b7dc-1cadd8181b57.jsonl")
            local session_id=$(basename "$transcript" .jsonl)
            local line_count=$(wc -l < "$transcript")
            local length_category=$(classify_length "$line_count")
            local intent_category=$(extract_intent "$session_id")
            local goal=$(extract_goal "$session_id")

            # Display session info
            echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
            print_stat "Project" "$readable_project"
            print_stat "Session ID" "${session_id:0:12}..."
            print_stat "Lines" "$line_count"
            print_stat "Category" "$length_category"
            print_stat "Intent" "$intent_category"
            if [[ -n "$goal" ]]; then
                print_stat "Goal" "${goal:0:60}$([ ${#goal} -gt 60 ] && echo '...')"
            fi

            # Show conversation preview
            echo ""
            echo "  Conversation preview:"
            local preview=$(extract_conversation_preview "$transcript")
            if [[ -n "$preview" ]]; then
                echo "$preview"
            else
                echo "      (no preview available)"
            fi

            # Prompt user
            echo ""
            read -p "Include this transcript? [y/N/q] " -n 1 -r
            echo

            if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Qq]$ ]]; then
                print_info "Quitting collection..."
                break 2
            fi

            if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
                # Optionally prompt for custom description
                local description="$goal"
                if [[ -z "$description" ]]; then
                    read -p "Enter brief description (or press Enter to skip): " description
                fi

                # Add to collection
                add_transcript "$session_id" "$project_dir" "$transcript" \
                              "$line_count" "$length_category" "$intent_category" \
                              "${description:-No description}"
            else
                print_info "Skipped"
            fi

            echo ""
            processed=$((processed + 1))
        done
    done

    # Generate metadata
    if [[ $count_total -gt 0 ]]; then
        write_metadata
        show_summary

        print_info "Test data ready in: $OUTPUT_DIR"
        print_info "Metadata file: $METADATA_FILE"
    else
        print_warning "No transcripts collected"
    fi

    echo ""
    print_info "Scanned $session_count sessions, processed $processed"
}

# Run main
main "$@"

#!/bin/bash
###############################################################################
# collect-test-data.sh
#
# Interactive script to collect diverse transcript samples for LLM benchmarking.
# Scans ~/.claude/projects/, displays stats, and prompts user to include/skip
# each transcript. Generates metadata.json with test case classifications.
#
# Usage:
#   ./scripts/collect-test-data.sh [--reset]
#
# Options:
#   --reset  Clear existing test data and start fresh
###############################################################################

set -euo pipefail

# Directories
PROJECTS_DIR="${HOME}/.claude/projects"
SIDEKICK_SESSIONS_DIR=".sidekick/sessions"
OUTPUT_DIR="test-data/transcripts"
METADATA_FILE="${OUTPUT_DIR}/metadata.json"

# Counters for distribution tracking
declare -i count_short=0
declare -i count_medium=0
declare -i count_long=0
declare -i count_total=0

# Array to store metadata entries
declare -a metadata_entries=()

###############################################################################
# Helper Functions
###############################################################################

# Print colored output
print_header() {
    echo ""
    echo "=============================================="
    echo "$1"
    echo "=============================================="
}

print_info() {
    echo "[INFO] $1"
}

print_stat() {
    printf "  %-20s %s\n" "$1:" "$2"
}

print_success() {
    echo "[✓] $1"
}

print_warning() {
    echo "[!] $1"
}

# Classify transcript by length
classify_length() {
    local lines=$1
    if (( lines < 50 )); then
        echo "short"
    elif (( lines <= 150 )); then
        echo "medium"
    else
        echo "long"
    fi
}


# Extract initial goal from sidekick topic.json if available
extract_goal() {
    local session_id=$1
    local topic_file="${SIDEKICK_SESSIONS_DIR}/${session_id}/topic.json"

    if [[ -f "$topic_file" ]]; then
        local goal=$(jq -r '.initial_goal // empty' "$topic_file" 2>/dev/null || echo "")
        if [[ -n "$goal" && "$goal" != "null" ]]; then
            echo "$goal"
            return
        fi
    fi
    echo ""
}

# Extract project name from encoded directory name
extract_project_name() {
    local dir_name=$1
    # Remove leading dash and convert remaining dashes to slashes for readability
    # E.g., "-home-scott-projects-claude-config" -> "home/scott/projects/claude-config"
    echo "${dir_name#-}" | sed 's/-/\//g'
}

# Preprocess transcript using same logic as topic-extraction.sh
# Filters tool messages and strips unnecessary attributes
preprocess_transcript() {
    local transcript=$1

    # Replicate topic-extraction.sh preprocessing:
    # 1. Extract .message field (ignore wrapper metadata)
    # 2. Filter out tool_use and tool_result content
    # 3. Strip model, id, type, stop_reason, stop_sequence, usage attributes
    jq -c '
        .message |
        select(. != null) |
        select(
            (.content |
                if type == "array" then
                    (.[0].type != "tool_use" and .[0].type != "tool_result")
                else
                    true
                end
            )
        ) |
        del(.model, .id, .type, .stop_reason, .stop_sequence, .usage)
    ' "$transcript" 2>/dev/null
}

# Extract conversation preview from preprocessed transcript
# Shows first 10 lines of cleansed messages
extract_conversation_preview() {
    local transcript=$1
    local preview=""
    local msg_count=0
    local max_messages=10

    # Preprocess and extract text
    while IFS= read -r message_json; do
        [[ -z "$message_json" ]] && continue

        # Extract role and content from preprocessed message
        local role=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.role // "unknown"')
        local content=""

        # Handle both string and array content
        local content_type=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.content | type')
        if [[ "$content_type" == "array" ]]; then
            # Extract text from array content (filter text type only)
            content=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.content[] | select(.type == "text") | .text' | head -1)
        else
            # String content
            content=$(echo "$message_json" | jq -r '.content')
        fi

        [[ -z "$content" || "$content" == "null" ]] && continue

        # Skip meta/command messages by content pattern
        [[ "$content" =~ ^\<(command|local-command|tool) ]] && continue
        [[ "$content" =~ ^Caveat:.*running\ local\ commands ]] && continue
        [[ ${#content} -lt 10 ]] && continue  # Skip very short messages

        # Capitalize role
        local display_role="${role^}"

        # Truncate long lines
        if [[ ${#content} -gt 90 ]]; then
            content="${content:0:87}..."
        fi

        preview+="      ${display_role}: ${content}"$'\n'
        msg_count=$((msg_count + 1))

        [[ $msg_count -ge $max_messages ]] && break
    done < <(preprocess_transcript "$transcript")

    echo "$preview"
}

# Generate a unique ID for the test case
generate_test_id() {
    local length_category=$1
    local counter=$2
    printf "%s-%03d" "$length_category" "$counter"
}

# Add a transcript to the collection
add_transcript() {
    local session_id=$1
    local session_dir=$2
    local transcript_file=$3
    local line_count=$4
    local length_category=$5
    local intent_category=$6
    local description=$7

    # Generate unique test ID
    local test_id
    case "$length_category" in
        short) test_id=$(generate_test_id "short" $((++count_short))) ;;
        medium) test_id=$(generate_test_id "medium" $((++count_medium))) ;;
        long) test_id=$(generate_test_id "long" $((++count_long))) ;;
    esac

    # Copy transcript to output directory
    local dest_file="${test_id}.jsonl"
    cp "$transcript_file" "${OUTPUT_DIR}/${dest_file}"

    # Create metadata entry
    local timestamp=$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")
    local entry=$(cat <<JSON
    {
      "id": "${test_id}",
      "file": "${dest_file}",
      "source_session": "${session_id}",
      "length_category": "${length_category}",
      "line_count": ${line_count},
      "intent_category": "${intent_category}",
      "description": "${description}",
      "collected_at": "${timestamp}"
    }
JSON
    )

    metadata_entries+=("$entry")
    count_total=$((count_total + 1))

    print_success "Added as ${test_id} (${length_category}, ${intent_category})"
}

# Generate final metadata.json
write_metadata() {
    print_info "Generating metadata.json..."

    # Join entries with commas
    local entries_json=""
    for i in "${!metadata_entries[@]}"; do
        if [[ $i -gt 0 ]]; then
            entries_json+=","
        fi
        entries_json+="${metadata_entries[$i]}"
    done

    # Write complete JSON
    cat > "$METADATA_FILE" <<JSON
{
  "dataset_version": "1.0",
  "generated_at": "$(date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ")",
  "test_count": ${count_total},
  "distribution": {
    "short": ${count_short},
    "medium": ${count_medium},
    "long": ${count_long}
  },
  "transcripts": [
${entries_json}
  ]
}
JSON

    print_success "Metadata written to ${METADATA_FILE}"
}

# Display collection summary
show_summary() {
    print_header "Collection Summary"
    print_stat "Total transcripts" "$count_total"
    print_stat "Short (<50 lines)" "$count_short ($(( count_short * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))%)"
    print_stat "Medium (50-150)" "$count_medium ($(( count_medium * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))%)"
    print_stat "Long (>150 lines)" "$count_long ($(( count_long * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))%)"
    echo ""

    # Check for balanced distribution
    local target_low=25
    local target_high=40
    local pct_short=$(( count_short * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))
    local pct_medium=$(( count_medium * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))
    local pct_long=$(( count_long * 100 / (count_total > 0 ? count_total : 1) ))

    if (( pct_short < target_low || pct_short > target_high )); then
        print_warning "Short transcripts: ${pct_short}% (target: 30-35%)"
    fi
    if (( pct_medium < target_low || pct_medium > target_high )); then
        print_warning "Medium transcripts: ${pct_medium}% (target: 30-40%)"
    fi
    if (( pct_long < target_low || pct_long > target_high )); then
        print_warning "Long transcripts: ${pct_long}% (target: 30-35%)"
    fi

    if (( count_total >= 20 && count_total <= 30 )); then
        print_success "Good sample size (20-30 recommended)"
    elif (( count_total < 20 )); then
        print_warning "Consider collecting more samples (20-30 recommended)"
    else
        print_info "Large sample set (${count_total} transcripts)"
    fi
}

###############################################################################
# Main Script
###############################################################################

main() {
    print_header "LLM Benchmark Test Data Collection"

    # Check for --reset flag
    if [[ "${1:-}" == "--reset" ]]; then
        print_warning "Resetting test data directory..."
        rm -f "${OUTPUT_DIR}"/*.jsonl "$METADATA_FILE"
        print_success "Existing test data cleared"
    fi

    # Verify projects directory exists
    if [[ ! -d "$PROJECTS_DIR" ]]; then
        echo "ERROR: Projects directory not found: $PROJECTS_DIR"
        exit 1
    fi

    # Verify output directory exists
    mkdir -p "$OUTPUT_DIR"

    # Check for existing metadata
    if [[ -f "$METADATA_FILE" ]]; then
        print_warning "Existing metadata.json found. Use --reset to start fresh."
        read -p "Continue and append? [y/N] " -n 1 -r
        echo
        if [[ ! $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
            echo "Aborted."
            exit 0
        fi
    fi

    print_info "Scanning projects directory: $PROJECTS_DIR"
    echo ""

    # Scan projects
    local session_count=0
    local processed=0

    for project_dir in "$PROJECTS_DIR"/*; do
        [[ -d "$project_dir" ]] || continue

        local project_name=$(basename "$project_dir")

        # Skip temporary Claude CLI projects
        if [[ "$project_name" == -tmp-* ]]; then
            continue
        fi

        local readable_project=$(extract_project_name "$project_name")

        # Scan transcripts in this project
        for transcript in "$project_dir"/*.jsonl; do
            [[ -f "$transcript" ]] || continue

            session_count=$((session_count + 1))

            # Extract session ID from filename (e.g., "51e06a7f-160e-4991-b7dc-1cadd8181b57.jsonl")
            local session_id=$(basename "$transcript" .jsonl)
            local line_count=$(wc -l < "$transcript")
            local length_category=$(classify_length "$line_count")
            local intent_category=$(extract_intent "$session_id")
            local goal=$(extract_goal "$session_id")

            # Display session info
            echo "━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━"
            print_stat "Project" "$readable_project"
            print_stat "Session ID" "${session_id:0:12}..."
            print_stat "Lines" "$line_count"
            print_stat "Category" "$length_category"
            print_stat "Intent" "$intent_category"
            if [[ -n "$goal" ]]; then
                print_stat "Goal" "${goal:0:60}$([ ${#goal} -gt 60 ] && echo '...')"
            fi

            # Show conversation preview
            echo ""
            echo "  Conversation preview:"
            local preview=$(extract_conversation_preview "$transcript")
            if [[ -n "$preview" ]]; then
                echo "$preview"
            else
                echo "      (no preview available)"
            fi

            # Prompt user
            echo ""
            read -p "Include this transcript? [y/N/q] " -n 1 -r
            echo

            if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Qq]$ ]]; then
                print_info "Quitting collection..."
                break 2
            fi

            if [[ $REPLY =~ ^[Yy]$ ]]; then
                # Optionally prompt for custom description
                local description="$goal"
                if [[ -z "$description" ]]; then
                    read -p "Enter brief description (or press Enter to skip): " description
                fi

                # Add to collection
                add_transcript "$session_id" "$project_dir" "$transcript" \
                              "$line_count" "$length_category" "$intent_category" \
                              "${description:-No description}"
            else
                print_info "Skipped"
            fi

            echo ""
            processed=$((processed + 1))
        done
    done

    # Generate metadata
    if [[ $count_total -gt 0 ]]; then
        write_metadata
        show_summary

        print_info "Test data ready in: $OUTPUT_DIR"
        print_info "Metadata file: $METADATA_FILE"
    else
        print_warning "No transcripts collected"
    fi

    echo ""
    print_info "Scanned $session_count sessions, processed $processed"
}

# Run main
main "$@"
